title: Improving General Role-Playing Agents via Psychology-Grounded Reasoning and Role-Aware Policy Optimization
abstract: arXiv:2606.27025v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Building general-purpose role-playing agents that faithfully portray any character from a natural-language profile remains challenging. The dominant paradigm -- supervised fine-tuning -- encourages behavioral mimicry without deep, human-like internal thought processes, resulting in poor out-of-distribution generalization. Therefore, we propose \textbf{Psy-CoT}, a psychology-grounded chain-of-thought framework that decomposes pre-response reasoning into three role-specific steps -- \emph{Interaction Perception}, \emph{Psychological Empathy}, and \emph{Logical Construction} -- so that the model \emph{thinks dynamically} from the profile rather than merely mimicking surface patterns. While structured reasoning provides a foundation, it alone is insufficient; reinforcement learning is essential to further align the model with character fidelity. However, we observe that under LLM-based reward models, both generic phrases that hack the reward

Title: Improving General Role-Playing Agents via Psychology-Grounded Reasoning and Role-Aware Policy Optimization
Abstract: arXiv:2606.27025v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Building general-purpose role-playing agents that faithfully portray any character from a natural-language profile remains challenging. The dominant paradigm -- supervised fine-tuning -- encourages behavioral mimicry without deep, human-like internal thought processes, resulting in poor out-of-distribution generalization. Therefore, we propose \textbf{Psy-CoT}, a psychology-grounded chain-of-thought framework that decomposes pre-response reasoning into three role-specific steps -- \emph{Interaction Perception}, \emph{Psychological Empathy}, and \emph{Logical Construction} -- so that the model \emph{thinks dynamically} from the profile rather than merely mimicking surface patterns. While structured reasoning provides a foundation, it alone is insufficient; reinforcement learning is essential to further align the model with character fidelity. However, we observe that under LLM-based reward models, both generic phrases that hack the reward
translated_title: 通过心理学驱动的推理与角色感知策略优化改进通用角色扮演智能体
translated_abstract: 构建能够根据自然语言描述忠实扮演任何角色的通用角色扮演智能体仍然具有挑战性。当前主导范式——监督微调——鼓励行为模仿，但缺乏深入、类似人类的内部思维过程，导致在分布外泛化能力差。因此，我们提出了**Psy-CoT**，一种基于心理学的思维链框架，它将响应前推理分解为三个角色特定的步骤——*交互感知*、*心理共情*和*逻辑构建*——使模型能够从角色描述出发进行*动态思考*，而不仅仅是模仿表面模式。虽然结构化推理提供了基础，但仅靠它是不够的；强化学习对于进一步使模型与角色保真度对齐至关重要。然而，我们观察到，在基于大语言模型的奖励模型下，那些为了获取奖励而使用的通用短语……
tldr: 提出一种结合心理学驱动的思维链推理与角色感知策略优化的方法，以提升通用角色扮演智能体的角色保真度和泛化能力。
en_tdlr: Proposes a method combining psychology-grounded chain-of-thought reasoning with role-aware policy optimization to improve character fidelity and generalization of general-purpose role-playing agents.
